 {"id":"bd-buvyq","title":"add mounted-path ext4 unlink-missing ENOENT coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:46:02.467058180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-bv3p5","title":"add ext4 quota inode read support","description":"Survey finding: ext4 superblock parsing includes quota inode numbers (s_usr_quota_inum, s_grp_quota_inum, s_prj_quota_inum) but there's no API to read quota data.

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_quota_info() or similar for reading quota limits/usage
- Parse quota file format from quota inodes
- Expose via inspect CLI or diagnostic API
- Add conformance test with mkfs -O quota image

Acceptance:
- Quota inode contents can be read and parsed
- Basic quota limits/usage exposed through API","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:13:18.207526452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:36:18.001605683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:36:17.998292821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-bx1wu","title":"[FUSE] memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB + MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL","description":"Surface MFD_HUGETLB and MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL semantics for memfd_create(2) when the FUSE-mounted filesystem is asked to back hugepage-allocated or sealed-against-exec memory files.

Context:
- memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB allocates the file from the hugetlbfs pool. The kernel routes this to hugetlbfs, not the FUSE backing store, so for ffs-fuse the only requirement is that we do not corrupt or refuse the call: the kernel handles it without invoking us.
- MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL (Linux 6.3+) implies MFD_ALLOW_SEALING and seals out PROT_EXEC mappings on the resulting fd. The seal lives on the inode and must persist across dup/sendmsg. For ffs-fuse, this means: F_GET_SEALS / F_ADD_SEALS via fcntl(2) must round-trip through FUSE_FALLOCATE-like ioctl plumbing or via FUSE_FCNTL once added.
- Reference: mm/memfd.c, fs/inode.c::file_seals, include/uapi/linux/memfd.h.

Most of MFD_HUGETLB does not touch the FUSE backend, but MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL needs ffs-inode to track a seal-flags bitmap on the in-memory Inode and reject mmap(PROT_EXEC) when the seal is set.

Acceptance:
- Test: memfd_create with MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL on a FUSE-mounted-backed location, then mmap with PROT_EXEC -> EACCES.
- Test: F_GET_SEALS after MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL returns F_SEAL_EXEC.
- xfstests generic/{562, 591} (memfd seal round-trip) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:11.177733601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:11.177733601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-bx1wu","title":"[FUSE] memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB + MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL","description":"Surface MFD_HUGETLB and MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL semantics for memfd_create(2) when the FUSE-mounted filesystem is asked to back hugepage-allocated or sealed-against-exec memory files.

Context:
- memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB allocates the file from the hugetlbfs pool. The kernel routes this to hugetlbfs, not the FUSE backing store, so for ffs-fuse the only requirement is that we do not corrupt or refuse the call: the kernel handles it without invoking us.
- MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL (Linux 6.3+) implies MFD_ALLOW_SEALING and seals out PROT_EXEC mappings on the resulting fd. The seal lives on the inode and must persist across dup/sendmsg. For ffs-fuse, this means: F_GET_SEALS / F_ADD_SEALS via fcntl(2) must round-trip through FUSE_FALLOCATE-like ioctl plumbing or via FUSE_FCNTL once added.
- Reference: mm/memfd.c, fs/inode.c::file_seals, include/uapi/linux/memfd.h.

Most of MFD_HUGETLB does not touch the FUSE backend, but MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL needs ffs-inode to track a seal-flags bitmap on the in-memory Inode and reject mmap(PROT_EXEC) when the seal is set.

Acceptance:
- Test: memfd_create with MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL on a FUSE-mounted-backed location, then mmap with PROT_EXEC -> EACCES.
- Test: F_GET_SEALS after MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL returns F_SEAL_EXEC.
- xfstests generic/{562, 591} (memfd seal round-trip) pass.","notes":"Investigating applicability before code changes. Kernel memfd_create takes only name+flags; need to verify whether any FUSE-mounted file can be involved before implementing inode-level seal state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:11.177733601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:10:20.427311298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:10:20.425026393Z","close_reason":"Not applicable to FrankenFS/FUSE as written. Linux memfd_create takes only a name and flags, not a path, mount, directory fd, or backing filesystem. Kernel mm/memfd.c allocates MFD_HUGETLB via hugetlb_file_setup and normal memfd via shmem_file_setup, then applies MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL/F_SEAL_EXEC to that anonymous memfd inode and handles F_ADD_SEALS/F_GET_SEALS through memfd_fcntl. No FUSE request is issued and no FrankenFS inode can back the object. If regular-file sealing over a mounted FUSE file is desired, that needs a separate fcntl/seal-support bead with real FUSE/kernel plumbing; this memfd_create bead's acceptance tests cannot be satisfied by changing FrankenFS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-bybe","title":"Remove debug tracing::error! calls from TransactionBlockAdapter","description":"TransactionBlockAdapter in ffs-core/src/lib.rs had tracing::error! debug calls (already removed by TopazHare). This bead is to verify no other leftover debug logging remains in production code paths.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:44.798241723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:48.143728119Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:48.143438631Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by TopazHare in this session","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Rules:
- every new behavior needs fixtures or properties
- parity numbers must match FEATURE_PARITY.md
- only optimize after correctness vectors exist

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c4vah","title":"add mounted-path btrfs hard-link refusal coverage for directory-source EPERM, non-directory parent ENOTDIR, and occupied destination EEXIST","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success, but not the refusal contracts that ffs-core already enforces for invalid link topologies. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that assert hard-linking a directory returns EPERM, linking into a non-directory parent returns ENOTDIR, and linking onto an occupied destination returns EEXIST, with no partial mutation of link counts or directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the public btrfs hard-link refusal surface for these three cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:48.235255693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.031360376Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.028106566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
